summary.title: Dataspace Control Plane — Company Onboarding Threat Model
summary.description: Threat model for the company onboarding flow: Operator -> web-console -> control-api -> OnboardingWorkflow (Temporal) -> Keycloak realm + Vault DID signing + Postgres tenant record.

data.name: Operator
data.description: Platform operator initiating company registration via the web-console

data.name: OnboardingWorkflow
data.description: Temporal durable workflow that orchestrates realm creation, DID signing, and tenant record persistence. Workflow history is the audit trail for onboarding.

threats.title: Duplicate realm creation via workflow replay
threats.description: If the OnboardingWorkflow is replayed (e.g., due to a Temporal worker restart mid-execution), the CreateRealm activity could attempt to create a realm that already exists, potentially overwriting its configuration
threats.mitigation: The CreateRealm activity is idempotent: it first checks if the realm exists (GET /realms/{realm}) and returns the existing realm if found. No overwrite occurs on replay.

threats.title: Workflow history leaks client_secret
threats.description: The CreateClient activity in Keycloak returns a client_secret. If this is stored as a workflow output or local variable visible in workflow history, it is exposed to anyone with Temporal UI access.
threats.mitigation: The client_secret is written to Vault immediately within the CreateClient activity and is never returned to the workflow as a result. Only the Vault secret path (not the secret value) is stored as the activity result in workflow history.

threats.title: Partial tenant record leaves orphaned Keycloak realm
threats.description: If the workflow fails after realm creation but before Postgres tenant record creation, a Keycloak realm exists without a corresponding tenant record. Re-running onboarding with the same legalEntityId would fail at the idempotency check.
threats.mitigation: Idempotent activity design: CreateRealm returns the existing realm on replay; InsertTenantRecord is guarded by an ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Temporal retry resumes from the last successful activity.

data.name: Keycloak
data.description: Identity provider. Receives realm and client creation requests from the OnboardingWorkflow via the Keycloak Admin REST API.

data.name: PostgreSQL
data.description: Receives the final tenant record INSERT after DID signing. RLS enabled.

data.name: Vault Transit (DID)
data.description: Signs the DID document for the new company. Key created with exportable=false during onboarding.
